heading: Commutation of rations for officers and crews of vessels of Service
label: 754 Commutation of rations for officers and crews of vessels of Service
text: On and after , commutation of rations (not to exceed $1 per man per day) may be paid to officers and crews of vessels of the United States Fish and Wildlife Service under regulations prescribed by the Secretary of the Interior, and money accruing from commutation of rations on board vessels may be paid on proper vouchers to the persons having charge of the mess of such vessels; and , shall not be construed to require deductions from the salaries of officers and crews of vessels of the United States Fish and Wildlife Service for quarters and rations furnished on vessels of said Service.July 2, 1942section 5911 of title 5
notes: Codification“” substituted in text for “the Act of ()” on authority of , , , the first section of which enacted Title 5, Government Organization and Employees.Section 5911 of title 55 U.S.C. 75aPub. L. 89–554, § 7(b)80 Stat. 631March 5, 1928Sept. 6, 1966
